goal_alignment.sparse_mla_single_gpu: Port/fuse the whole sparse-MLA backward boundary so the optimized body consumes q, dO, compressed/shared KV, LI indices, output, lse/sink directly instead of acting as a late dKV drop-in.
goal_alignment.moe_mori_multi_gpu: Optimize MORI EP/MoE at 4x/8x MI350 and promote only sustained e2e TFLOPs plus memory, not isolated kernels or step-2 bursts.

current_best_isolated_dkv_slice.candidate: TileLang actual-LI selected-limit query-major MFMA body
current_best_isolated_dkv_slice.read: This beats the older 8.0-8.8 ms sparse-atomic compressed-dKV wall, but it is not materialized into e2e TFLOPs because the integrated path still pays source/HSK/probability bridge costs.

rejected_whole_boundary_shapes.reason: Consumes source tensors but uses raw per-selected dKV atomics; not enough write reduction and unstable e2e.
Still writes BF16 HSK probability/scaled-dP slabs through HBM and had non-clean correctness/e2e durability.
No-HSK, but group-owned contribution production destroys query-major q/dO reuse.
Reduced writes but segment-owned scalar recompute loses MFMA/query-major reuse.

next_implementation_gate.name: query_major_no_hsk_segmented_dkv_producer
contract: Keep query-major row recompute for QK, probabilities, scaled dP, dQ, and dKV contribution math.
Do not write probs_hsk or grad_logits_scaled_hsk slabs to HBM.
Do not make compressed group the outer owner if that reloads/recomputes Q/dO across groups.
Inside the query-major producer, add a small keyed/segmented reducer for compressed group within the token/panel tile.
Emit reduced compact partial rows or reduced atomics, then use the already-cheap final fold/reduce.
next_implementation_gate.first_perf_gate: Beat 42.5 ms source-boundary atomic-dKV at S4096, then beat 8.0-8.8 ms for the compressed-dKV slice without HSK slabs.
next_implementation_gate.promotion_gate: Only run an e2e TFLOPs A/B after the whole-boundary S4096 path is faster than the current retained explicit/bridge path.

skew_read.interpretation: The largest combine_dx waits are on ranks with tiny local VJP work, while the owner-rank VJP tail is on different ranks with large recv counts. That keeps the target as owner VJP tail reduction and earlier dX overlap, not raw MORI combine kernel tuning.

rejected_or_non_promoted_branches.decision: Do not promote for EP8; regresses below the retained 64.91 TFLOPs/GPU 8x anchor.
Correctness viable but not a durable promotion.
Route identity is validated, but it needs much larger MORI heap at long sequence and is slower than sync in diagnostic shapes.
Avoids VJP recompute but replaces the AITER fused forward with a slow padded forward, so it loses e2e.

next_implementation_gate.name: aiter_forward_exposes_training_intermediates_or_fused_owner_vjp
contract: Keep AITER fused forward as the forward floor; do not replace it with padded_saved PyTorch-like forward.
Expose or recompute inside an AITER/CK/TileLang training backward the useful intermediates: gate, up, hidden, and compact expert output.
Reduce owner VJP tail first: recompute_forward, wgrad, xgrad_reduce, and w2_dgrad_swiglu are the largest local pieces.
Only revisit AsyncLL/split combine after dX is produced early enough to overlap with wgrad without duplicating route-expanded buffers.
next_implementation_gate.first_perf_gate: Beat the EP8 owner-rank padded VJP baseline and the ideal saved-intermediate envelope of about 4.96-5.50 ms locally without slowing forward.
next_implementation_gate.promotion_gate: Run 4x S4096 GBS64 for at least 6 steps, then 8x S4096 GBS128 for at least 6 steps; promote by late steps 4-6 TFLOPs/GPU and peak memory only.

commands_run: sed -n on experiment README.md and recipe.yaml
